status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 8 / REFERENCE-SEQUENCE OR IDENTITY-CONFLICT CONTROL ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Supplier-named prostate short regulatory peptide family
mechanism_summary: No canonical mechanism is assigned. The product name suggests an organ-targeted commercial positioning, but exact sequence, target and pharmacology require direct primary-source and analytical closure.
evidence_maturity: Regional short-peptide, cell, animal or limited human literature may exist under this name or related names. Evidence is not attributed to the catalogue product until chemical identity and source linkage are confirmed.
regulatory_headline: IDENTITY-SENSITIVE SUPPLIER PRODUCT FAMILY / NO UNIVERSAL APPROVAL OR ORGAN-TREATMENT CLAIM
certification_blockers: Molecular formula, mass and reference standard
Current product- and jurisdiction-specific regulatory status
closure_class: KEDP TETRAPEPTIDE / PROSTATE POSITIONING AND SUPPLIER EQUIVALENCE
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — NLM/PUBCHEM IDENTITY LYS-GLU-ASP-PRO (KEDP) AND LIMITED CHROMATIN/ORGANOTYPIC LITERATURE IDENTIFIED; MAX20 TERMINI, SALT, ANALYTICAL IDENTITY, STERILITY AND CONTROLLED HUMAN BENEFIT OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: PubChem records Prostamax as Lys-Glu-Asp-Pro (KEDP). Indexed literature includes prostate-tissue organotypic models and human-lymphocyte chromatin studies, but does not establish treatment of benign prostatic hyperplasia, cancer, urinary symptoms or supplier MAX20 equivalence.
critical_closure_controls: KEDP database identity is not MAX20 analytical proof
Prostate-targeted positioning is not clinical selectivity
Chromatin and organotypic findings are not treatment evidence
No BPH, prostate-cancer, urinary or sexual-health claim
No dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
